name: Consignor / exporter creation (address form)
url: /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/{chedReference}/traders/consignee/new?reimport=true — OBSERVED: reached via the consignor/exporter creation flow (Add a consignor or exporter -> Create a new consignor or exporter), yet the rendered form posts to and carries a returnUrl of .../traders/consignee/new?reimport=true. The consignor creation flow renders the SAME 'consignee' address form (see openQuestions[0]); there is no evidence of a distinct /traders/consignor/new page in this trace.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Every class under main is govuk-* (govuk-heading-xl, govuk-body, govuk-link, govuk-form-group, govuk-label, govuk-label--m, govuk-input, govuk-!-width-one-half, govuk-fieldset, govuk-fieldset__legend, govuk-fieldset__legend--m, govuk-select, govuk-button, govuk-grid-row, govuk-grid-column-full).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: No non-standard markup on this page — it is 100% inside the GOV.UK Design System toolbox. Directly rebuildable with govuk-frontend components; the country <select> should source its list from a reference-data service rather than hardcoding.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 91 (structure eval — every listed class is govuk-*)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: consignor / consignee creation render an identical, consignee-labelled form
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The consignor/exporter creation flow (Add a consignor or exporter -> Create a new consignor or exporter, driven by ConsignorCreationPage.ts) renders a form whose H1 is "Add consignee", whose name label is "Consignee name", whose inputs carry title="consignee", and which posts to (and carries a returnUrl of) .../traders/consignee/new?reimport=true. The consignee creation flow renders the same form. The two trader types are NOT distinguished by any visible copy on this page in this trace.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Legacy IPAFFS reuses/mislabels the address-creation template so the consignor page is titled and labelled 'consignee'. The new IUU service MUST label the consignor/exporter page correctly (heading and name label should name the actual trader role being added) rather than copying this shared-template behaviour.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Distinct, correctly-labelled page headings and field labels per trader role (still standard govuk-heading-xl / govuk-label components).
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d actions 85-96 (consignor flow) — runtime action logs 87 and 91 show target inputs with title="consignee"; snapshot 87/91 form action + hidden returnUrl = .../traders/consignee/new?reimport=true; frozen values at action 91 (name=Linus George Ltd, address-line-1=101 Main St) differ from the empty consignee form at action 100, proving no snapshot bleed.

fields.name: company-name
fields.label: Consignee name
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 87 (Fill 'Linus George Ltd' in the CONSIGNOR flow) + action 91 DOM (control inventory eval id=company-name; structure eval label = 'Consignee name'). Label is 'Consignee name' EVEN in the consignor/exporter creation flow — confirmed via the runtime log of action 87 (locator getByRole textbox name 'name' resolved to <input id=company-name title="consignee" ...>) and the frozen structure at action 91. The page object (ConsignorCreationPage.ts:48) uses a loose accessible-name substring 'name' precisely because the label 'Consignee name' contains the word 'name' — this does NOT imply a different consignor label; no separate 'Consignor or exporter name' label exists in the observed evidence. observedValue 'Linus George Ltd' is the consignorAddress name. No HTML required attr; mandatory enforcement is server-side and no test asserts a validation message for this field.

fields.label: Address line 1

fields.name: address-line-3

Address line 1, city/town, postcode carry NO '(optional)' suffix, so are inferred mandatory — but no test drives an empty value to prove it (validation not exercised) = gap on the exact required set.

The rendered <select> is GB-nations-only. sourceCorroboration.pageObject: ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/notification/ConsignorCreationPage.ts (consignor variant) and ConsigneeCreationPage.ts (consignee variant) — the two share an identical control set. Observed reality: driven by ConsignorCreationPage.ts, the consignor/exporter creation flow renders the consignee-labelled form (H1 'Add consignee', label 'Consignee name', title="consignee", url traders/consignee/new). The consignor page object's use of a loose accessible-name substring 'name' for the name field is because the label 'Consignee name' contains 'name' — not because the consignor label differs.
sourceCorroboration.fieldSetComplete: Nine visible controls captured from the trace DOM — company-name, address-line-1, address-line-2, address-line-3, city-or-town, postcode, telephone, country (select), email — plus the 'Save and continue' button and six hidden inputs (crumb x2, etag, reimport, returnUrl, fromFooterHeader). No additional visible control observed.
sourceCorroboration.domainType: The Address domain shape backing the fill is exactly these nine fields, all string-typed (inferred from the observed control set; no extra address attribute rendered).
sourceCorroboration.journeyPosition: Reached during trader assignment: Traders/addresses task -> Add a consignor or exporter (action 85) -> Create a new consignor or exporter (action 86) -> this address form (actions 87-96) -> Save and continue (96) -> Add to notification (97). The consignee equivalent follows immediately (actions 98-100+). Position within the CHED-P fish (IUU) notification journey.
sourceCorroboration.noTestAssertions: No functional ched-p test asserts any label, copy or validation message for this page. The workflow only fills-and-continues; the a11y test fills and checks accessibility only — so all validation copy remains a gap, not merely unobserved.

openQuestions: CONFIRMED (not a gap): the consignor/exporter creation flow renders the SAME 'Add consignee' form as the consignee flow. Action 91 is inside the consignor block (action 85 'Add a consignor or exporter' -> 86 'Create a new consignor or exporter', source ConsignorCreationPage.ts:48/52), yet the rendered H1 is 'Add consignee', the name label is 'Consignee name', inputs carry title="consignee", and the form posts to (returnUrl) .../traders/consignee/new?reimport=true. Proven distinct from the later consignee form by differing frozen values (action 91 name='Linus George Ltd'/address-line-1='101 Main St' vs empty at action 100), so this is NOT snapshot bleed. The earlier framing that the trace 'accidentally captured the consignee variant and a consignor trace is needed' is a MISDIAGNOSIS: this IS the consignor trace, and it shows consignee copy. Open item for humans: is this a genuine IPAFFS mislabel/shared-template bug, and how should the new IUU service correctly title/label the consignor vs consignee pages?

Country <select> option set: both the trace and every test that drives either variant only ever use England ('GB-ENG'). The rendered dropdown is GB-nations-only (England/Scotland/Wales/Northern Ireland + placeholder). Whether a fuller country reference-data list appears on any unexercised code path is a gap — treat the list as reference data sourced from a service either way.

Required set: address lines 2 and 3 are explicitly optional (label '(optional)'). Name, address line 1, city/town, postcode, country, telephone and email carry no optional marker and no HTML required attr; which are actually mandatory (and their validation copy) is server-side and was never exercised — a gap for a human.

No field carries an HTML required attribute; mandatory-field enforcement and validation copy are server-side and were not exercised in this zero-error trace and are asserted by no test — validation messages are a gap.
No hint text, error summary or error message on the page (0 .govuk-hint, 0 .govuk-error-summary, 0 .govuk-error-message at action 91).
